item.title: Trump lauds "very good meetings" as indirect U.S.-Iran talks resume
item.description: Vance says the U.S. has accomplished its "core mission" in Iran, as Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner meet mediators in Qatar, but not Iranians.

item.title: Dog pulled from rubble 5 days after Venezuela quakes, video shows
item.description: Dramatic video showed the moment a rescue team pulled a small dog from the rubble nearly a week after twin earthquakes​ devastated Venezuela.

item.title: Victor Willis, Village People lead singer, dies at 74
item.description: Victor Willis, lead singer of the disco group Village People, whose hit "Y.M.C.A." became a fixture at rallies for President Trump, has died at the age of 74, his wife and the band said.

item.title: Can creditors charge interest on unpaid debts after someone dies?
item.description: When it comes to interest, what happens after death depends on the debt, the estate and the settlement timeline.

item.title: Actor Danny Glover reveals Alzheimer's diagnosis
item.description: Actor Danny Glover spoke about his Alzheimer's diagnosis in an interview with NBC's "Today" show, revealing that he has been living with the disease for several years.

item.title: Sony to stop making physical discs for PlayStation starting in 2028
item.description: After January 2028, new games will be available exclusively in digital format from the PlayStation Store and at retailers, Sony said.

item.title: USMNT "ready to run through a wall" ahead of World Cup knockout stage, Charlie Davies says
item.description: The U.S. men's national soccer team performed well in the group stage of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, but they haven't won a knockout match since 2002. "It's the pressure time," former USMNT player Charlie Davies said.

item.title: Colorado's Melat Kiros poised to become first Gen Z member of Congress after primary win
item.description: Melat Kiros, the Democratic socialist candidate who won the primary for Colorado's 1st Congressional District, could become the first Gen Z member of Congress. CBS News' Taurean Small has the latest.

item.title: Vance says Iran war will have "win-win outcome for the American people" regardless of negotiations
item.description: Vice President JD Vance says that the U.S. has "all the cards in the negotiation" with Iran. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean weighs in.

item.title: Texas Republican chair says Trump will galvanize voters with first-ever GOP midterm convention
item.description: President Trump announced that Republicans will hold a midterm convention in Texas as his party ramps up efforts to sway voters and maintain power through the 2026 election. Texas GOP chair D'rinda Randall joins CBS News with more on the event.

item.title: Harvard professor known for alien theories to lead White House UFO council
item.description: Avi Loeb, a Harvard astronomer who has made headlines with his theories about alien encounters, will lead a White House scientific advisory council on UFOs.

item.title: From the archives: Charles Kuralt on the Fourth of July
item.description: In 1990 the "Sunday Morning" host headlined a CBS special primetime broadcast, "On the Fourth of July with Charles Kuralt," remembering the many ways in which our nation marks its birthday. Enjoy this excerpt (rebroadcast on "Sunday Morning" July 1, 2007) marking Independence Day celebrations – parades and tubing down lazy rivers, axe throws and greased pig contests – from sea to shining sea.

item.title: Toddler pulled from rubble after Venezuela earthquakes
item.description: Search-and-rescue workers in Venezuela are still combing through wreckage after two earthquakes devastated the region. Despite a rising death toll, there are still glimmers of hope. Emergency management professional Carlos Castillo joins CBS News with more on the search.

item.title: Trump takes first trip on Qatari-gifted Air Force One
item.description: President Trump is traveling Wednesday to North Dakota in what marks his first trip aboard the new Air Force One, which was gifted to the U.S. by the Qatari government last year.

item.title: Trump made over $1 billion on crypto ventures last year, disclosure shows
item.description: President Trump earned more than a billion dollars from crypto-related ventures alone last year, according to a financial disclosure, including from his meme coin business and his family's cryptocurrency firm.

item.title: What types of debt payments can you negotiate?
item.description: Not every debt payment is set in stone. Here's where negotiating could help lower what you owe.

item.title: Your beach and pool safety guide amid intense heat
item.description: Amid the scorching temperatures, you may be headed to the beach or pool to try to cool off. Ash-har Quraishi has water safety tips and shows how the National Weather Service is helping to secure beaches.

item.title: 200 million under extreme heat alerts
item.description: Around 200 million people in the U.S. are under extreme heat alerts on Wednesday. Rob Marciano has the latest forecast in the lead-up to the Fourth of July holiday.

item.title: Americans brace for busy airports, high gas prices as holiday travel rush looms
item.description: An estimated 72 million Americans will travel 50 miles or more during the 4th of July holiday weekend, AAA predicts. CBS News' Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Millions of Americans could experience hottest July 4th ever
item.description: Extremely dangerous heat, coupled with humidity, could result in heat index readings of 100 to 115 degrees from the Midwest to the East Coast, forecasters said.

item.title: How you can handle changes to the federal student loan program
item.description: Starting on Wednesday, the Department of Education is sending out notices to some federal student loan borrowers with important deadlines. CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger explains who is affected and what to do if you receive a notice.

item.title: Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding celebration expected in NYC on Thursday, sources say
item.description: New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani teased Taylor Swift's rumored wedding at Madison Square Garden, sending Swifties into a frenzy. In addition, two law enforcement sources say the celebrity couple plans to have a rehearsal dinner Thursday evening and a wedding celebration on Friday. CBS News' Jo Ling Kent has the latest.

item.title: Trans athlete's lawyer on Supreme Court decision: "Bad for any woman"
item.description: Lawyer Camilla B. Taylor, who represents transgender athlete Becky Pepper-Jackson, says the teen "never experienced male puberty at all," as she reacts to claims that trans athletes can have a potential strength advantage on sports teams. Taylor joined "CBS News Mornings" after the Supreme Court decision that determined states can uphold bans on trans athletes in women's sports.

item.title: What's next for LeBron James, Serena Williams?
item.description: Sports journalist and 60 Minutes correspondent Jon Wertheim breaks down what's next for LeBron James and where he may end up after announcing his departure from the Lakers. He also discusses the future for tennis star Serena Williams, who made her comeback at Wimbledon.

item.title: New student loan rules take effect today. Here's what to know.
item.description: Loan revamp affects how much students and families can borrow to pay for college, as well as their repayment options.

item.title: Progressive Manny Rutinel projected to win the Democratic primary in CD-8
item.description: Progressive Manny Rutinel is the projected winner in the Democratic primary in Colorado's 8th Congressional District, a GOP-held seat that is one of a handful of toss-up districts in the country.

item.title: Medicare will now help pay for some GLP-1 drugs. Here's what to know
item.description: Starting Wednesday, Medicare will help pay for some GLP-1 medications for weight loss. CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Celine Gounder explains what this means for patients.

item.title: Woman survives falling 1,500 feet down Mount Shasta
item.description: A woman suffered injuries but survived falling 1,500 feet down California's Mount Shasta, which she had attempted to climb, officials said.

item.title: Labor activist Dolores Huerta says Trump "does not know history"
item.description: Longtime labor activist Dolores Huerta says President Trump's disparaging remarks about Mexicans show he "does not know history," and called on Latinos in California, Texas and other states to push for change.

item.title: New disease threats follow Trump administration's health program cuts
item.description: From screwworm to flesh-eating bacteria, mounting public health risks are emerging in the wake of deep cuts to federal health agencies and programs.
